findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.lean` defines `class SubNegMonoid ... extends AddMonoid G, Neg G, Sub G` with `sub_eq_add_neg`, and `class AddGroup ... extends SubNegMonoid`; lines 115-117 and 129-131 use only `hstar` and `sub_eq_add_neg`.
findings.fix: Generalize the `_sub` variables/theorems to `[SubNegMonoid G] [StarAddMonoid G]` (which still supplies the `AddMonoid` needed by `IsPositiveDefinite`) and keep the existing `rw [hstar, ← sub_eq_add_neg]` proof shape.
findings.issue: `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_sub` and the iff version below require `[AddGroup G]`, but their statements/proofs only use the subtraction API `a - b = a + -b`; the group inverse laws are unused.

summary: The main function-kernel equivalence is at the natural level, but the subtraction corollaries assume an additive group where Mathlib has a weaker standard subtraction class that suffices.
